languageBack日本語DeutschEnglishEspañolPortuguêsFrançaisHigh ContrastLog inHomeWebsiteWhat are AI authoring tools? (And should you use one?)What are AI authoring tools? (And should you use one?)Mohd Wasim ChoudhurySEO & CONTENT STRATEGY TOOLImprove your content strategy with HubSpot's content & SEO planning tool.Learn MoreUpdated:05/08/23Content production before AI often required hours of research, drafting, editing, and optimization, with even experienced writers spending hours crafting a single blog post. With the rise of AI content writing tools, marketers now approach content creation more strategically. According to HubSpot's2026 State of Marketing report, marketers save an average of 3 hours per piece of content when using generative AI tools.The best AI writing tools now go beyond basic text generation. Modern platforms offerSEO optimization, brand voice consistency, plagiarism detection, and integration with content management systems.Content Hub, a CMS, includes AI-powered writing assistance through Breeze by HubSpot, helping marketing teams align SEO suggestions with AEO requirements.This guide tests 12 leading AI content writing tools across pricing, features, and real-world performance to help marketing teams find the right solution for their workflow.Table of ContentsWhat are AI Writing Tools?What Can AI Writing Tools Do?12 AI Content Writing Tools to Try:AI Writing Tools Pricing & Features ComparisonFrequently Asked Questions About AI Writing ToolsFinding the Perfect AI Writing PartnerWhat are AI Writing Tools?AI writing toolsare software platforms that use artificial intelligence to generate, edit, and optimize written content. These platforms leverage natural language processing (NLP), machine learning algorithms, and large language models (LLMs) to understand context, maintain tone consistency, and produce human-quality text across various formats.The difference between AI writing and traditional writing is stark. Manual content creation demands extensive research, multiple rounds of revision, and countless hours staring at a blank cursor. AI-powered authoring tools skip the paralysis entirely by generating content that follows proven structures and copywriting formulas that actually work.Free AI Content WriterIdeate, create, and share content. Easily create...Blog articles.Website copy.Social posts.And more!Get started freeAI Models vs. AI Writing ToolsAI ModelsAI Writing ToolsWhat they areCore AI engines that generate text responsesApplications built on AI models for content creationExamplesGPT-4, Claude, GeminiJasper, Copy.ai, WritesonicPrimary roleProduce human-like languageStreamline content workflowsEase of useRequires careful promptingDesigned for quick, guided useContent focusGeneral-purpose generationMarketing and content-specific tasksSEO supportNot included by defaultBuilt-in SEO analysis and optimizationTemplatesNonePre-built templates for blogs, ads, and emailsBrand voice controlManual and inconsistentStructured and repeatablePlagiarism checkingNot availableBuilt-in plagiarism detectionCollaborationSingle-user focusedTeam workflows and approvalsCMS integrationNot supportedDirect publishing and integrationsBest suited forExperimentation and flexibilityScalable content productionView all rowsView fewer rowsTypes of AI Writing SoftwareAI content writing toolsserve several purposes, such as generating email and ad copy, assisting with long-form blog posts, optimizing for SEO, and supporting creative marketing strategies. Since different AI authoring tools tackle specific content challenges, they’re unique and distinct from one another.Content GeneratorsThink of content generators as a personal writing team that never sleeps. Marketers can type in a topic and a rough outline, and watch them spin out blog posts, whitepapers, or ebooks faster than anyone can brew their morning coffee. These powerhouses excel at transforming skeletal outlines into coherent first drafts, handling the heavy lifting so marketers can focus on refinement rather than staring at blank pages.Writing AssistantsAI content writing tools don‘t generate content from scratch. Instead, they’re polish-and-perfect partners. AI authoring tools catch passive voice creeping into third paragraphs, suggest punchier alternatives to overused words, and flag sentences that make readers work too hard. It's like having a grammar expert and style coach working overtime to make writing crisp, clear, and compelling.SEO ToolsCreating brilliant content means nothing if no one finds it. That's where SEO tools come into play. These AI-powered authoring tools are like having an SEO expert analyzing content in real time to check keyword placement, evaluate heading structure, measure readability, and spot opportunities to strengthen internal links.The best part? Modern SEO AI authoring tools now optimize for AI-generated search results, helping content stay visible as search engines evolve. AI SEO tools turn the mystery of “what ranks” into actionable insights that marketers can actually use.Creative ToolsCreative Tools are the sprinters of the AI writing world, cranking out snappy ad headlines, scroll-stopping social posts, and click-worthy email subject lines at lightning speed. Built for marketers who need dozens of variations for testing, these tools speak fluent AIDA and PAS, turning conversion psychology into compelling micro-content.How AI Writing Tools Compare to ChatGPTChatGPT provides broad, conversational AI and generates content from prompts, but AI content writing tools are built specifically for professional content workflows. Some AI content writing tools include pre-built templates for multiple content formats, removing the need for detailed prompt engineering that ChatGPT requires.A handful of these AI authoring tools integrate directly with platforms like WordPress and HubSpot, streamlining the drafting, editing, and publishing process. Multi-user collaboration enables team editing, version control, and approvals, while offering real-time SEO scoring during writing to evaluate keywords, structure, and search intent instantly.What Can AI Writing Tools Do?AI authoring tools serve multiple purposes across the content creation lifecycle, from ideation through optimization. Understanding their capabilities helps marketing teams identify which features deliver the most value for their specific content needs and workflows.Generate Blog PostsAI-powered authoring tools reduce blog writing time by generating structured outlines, complete first drafts, and comprehensive articles that cover key subtopics. These platforms analyze topic keywords, research competitors‘ content, identify content gaps, and produce articles that answer readers’ questions.For content teams producing 10+ articles monthly, this capability transforms productivity. Instead of staring at a blank page, writers receive a solid foundation to build upon. The AI handles initial research, structures arguments logically, and maintains a consistent tone across thousands of words.According to the HubSpot 2026State of Marketing report, 80.5% of marketers are now using AI for content creation, with 42.4% using it extensively. This widespread adoption reflects the fact that AI authoring tools have become essential for modern content teams.The top AI tools for content writing allow users to specify parameters like target word count, desired tone (professional, casual, authoritative), and target audience expertise level. Advanced tools analyze top-ranking content for the target keyword and ensure the generated article covers the same depth of topics while adding unique perspectives.The result is faster content output. Writers then refine the output, add brand-specific examples, incorporate data from proprietary research, and polish the narrative voice.What to Look For:Template variety for different post types (listicles, how-to guides, comparison articles, thought leadership), outline generation quality before full draft creation, ability to specify word count and tone preferences, research capabilities that pull relevant statistics and examples, and integration with content management systems for seamless publishing.Create Ads and Social CopyShort-form content creation is where many AI content writing tools demonstrate their greatest strength. These platforms understand proven copywriting formulas and can craft conversion-focused messaging across multiple channels and formats.AI authoring tools trained on high-performing ad campaigns recognize patterns that drive clicks and conversions. They generate headlines that capture attention and calls to action that motivate reader response.Savvy marketers are already using AI to improve performance. The HubSpot 2026State of Marketingreport shows 67.4% of marketers use AI for campaign performance auditing and optimization, helping them refine ad copy based on real-time performance data.Social media content creation benefits particularly from AI assistance. Platforms can adapt the same core message across Twitter‘s character limits, LinkedIn’s professional tone, Instagram‘s visual-first approach, and Facebook’s engagement-focused format. This cross-platform adaptation saves hours of manual rewriting.Beyond that, AI can be used to make campaigns more personal. HubSpot found that68.5% leverage AI for personalized content experiences, enabling them to tailor messaging to individual prospects and customer segments at scale.The proficiency in short-form content makes AI authoring tools valuable for email marketing campaigns, Google Ads copy, Facebook and Instagram ad variations, Twitter threads, LinkedIn posts, and product description pages that require persuasive, scannable copy.What to Look For:Platform-specific formatting that respects character limits and best practices for each channel, A/B testing capabilities that generate variations for performance comparison, conversion-focused templates based on proven frameworks, hook generators for attention-grabbing openings, and integration with advertising platforms for streamlined campaign creation.On-Page OptimizationSEO-focused AI content writing tools analyze content structure and provide real-time feedback to improve search visibility. These tools evaluate dozens of ranking factors simultaneously and offer actionable recommendations as users create content. According to HubSpot,70.6% of marketersuse AI for SEO across both traditional and AI-powered search engines.On-page optimization features examine heading hierarchy to ensure proper H2 and H3 structure, keyword placement and density to avoid over-optimization, content depth compared to top-ranking competitors, internal linking opportunities to other relevant pages, readability scores using established metrics like Flesch-Kincaid, and meta description quality to improve click-through rates.Advanced platforms now include answer engine optimization (AEO) features designed to help content appear in AI-generated responses from tools such as ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Google's AI Overviews.Additionally, recent research shows thatarticles over 2,900 words average 5.1 citations in AI responses, while those under 800 words get only3.2 citations, emphasizing the importance of comprehensive content depth for AI visibility.Real-time scoring provides immediate feedback during the writing process. Instead of drafting content and then optimizing it later, writers see how each paragraph affects overall SEO performance. This integrated approach produces stronger content in less time.For content teams managing SEO at scale across hundreds of articles, automated optimization saves hours of manual analysis while improving ranking potential. HubSpot's Content Hub with Breeze combines SEO and AEO optimization features within an integrated CMS platform, allowing teams to write, optimize, and publish without switching between multiple tools.What to Look For:Real-time SEO scoring visible during the writing process, keyword density analysis that prevents over-optimization, competitor content comparison showing gaps in topic coverage, readability metrics with specific improvement suggestions, AEO optimization features for AI search visibility, and content brief generation based on top-ranking pages.Generate TaglinesBrand messaging and tagline creation typically require multiple brainstorming sessions, creative revisions, and rounds of stakeholder feedback. AI authoring tools accelerate this process by generating dozens of options based on brand values, target audience characteristics, and desired emotional response.These platforms use established copywriting frameworks and pattern recognition from successful brand campaigns to create memorable, concise messaging. They understand the rhythm and structure that make taglines stick in memory, incorporating techniques like alliteration, rhyme, parallel structure, and punchy brevity.AI tools can generate taglines across different approaches: benefit-focused statements that highlight customer value, emotion-driven phrases that connect with audience feelings, differentiation-based messaging that sets brands apart, and aspiration-oriented taglines that inspire action.What to Look For:Brand voice customization that reflects company personality, industry-specific templates that understand sector nuances, emotional tone controls for different audience segments, variation generation that builds on successful concepts, and the ability to specify tagline length and structure preferences.Ready-Made TemplatesPre-built content templates eliminate the blank page problem by providing structured frameworks for common content types. Leading AI writing platforms offer hundreds of templates that codify proven content structures, ensuring consistency and reducing formatting time.Template libraries typically include blog post introductions that hook readers immediately, email newsletters with optimal structure for engagement, and product descriptions that highlight features and benefits.Some templates offer video scripts with proper pacing and call-to-action placement, FAQ sections that address common customer questions, and press release formats that follow journalistic standards.Whereas other templates, like case studies, showcase customer success, the purpose of landing page copy is typically conversion, social media post frameworks for different platforms, and ad copy templates for various campaign objectives.These templates aren't rigid forms but rather starting points that teams customize to match brand guidelines and content goals. Organizations can save successful content as custom templates, creating a library of proven formats for recurring content types.The consistency that templates provide is particularly valuable for distributed content teams. When multiple writers use the same template structure, the resulting content maintains a unified voice and format even across different authors.What to Look For:Template variety spanning content types and formats, customization options that allow brand-specific adjustments, ability to save and share custom templates across teams, regular updates with new template formats based on content trends, and preview functionality that shows template structure before generation.12 AI Content Writing Tools to Try:The AI content writing landscape in 2025 isn‘t one-size-fits-all, and that’s actually good news for marketers.Instead of a single “best” tool, marketers now have specialized solutions that excel at specific tasks. Whether crafting SEO articles, managing social media, or building an entire content strategy, there's an AI tool designed for every workflow.Best Overall AI Writing ToolsThis category highlights tools that provide balanced capabilities across content ideation, drafting, editing, and publishing. These tools are excellent for creators who need reliable performance across formats without learning complex workflows. They combine quality output with adaptability, making them suitable for bloggers, marketers, and business professionals looking for an all-purpose AI writing partner.HubSpot Breeze Content AssistantHubSpot Breeze Content Assistant is an AI authoring tool integrated within theHubSpot CRM and marketing platform. It generates content for blogs, emails, landing pages, and social media posts while leveraging CRM data for personalization and maintaining consistency across marketing campaigns.Key FeaturesCRM integration leverages customer data, interaction history, and segmentation for personalized content creation across marketing channelsMulti-format content generates blog posts, emails, landing pages, social media posts, and ad copy within the HubSpot platformBrand voice consistency maintains tone and messaging alignment across all content types and marketing campaignsSEO optimization integrates with HubSpot's SEO tools to ensure content follows best practices for search visibilityWhat I LikeExcellent integration with HubSpot's marketing platformCRM-powered personalization for targeted contentStreamlined workflow from creation to publicationConsistent brand voice across marketing channelsIn my experience, HubSpot Content Assistant with CRM data is its standout feature. It can reference customer information, campaign data, and previous interactions to create personalized content that aligns with specific marketing strategies.When I used the Content Assistant within HubSpot's CMS, the tool maintained brand voice consistency and automatically optimized content for SEO. The seamless workflow from writing to publishing eliminated the need to copy content between platforms.For email campaigns, Content Assistant leveraged contact properties and list segmentation to suggest personalized messaging that resonated with different audience segments, which significantly improved my engagement rates.Best for:HubSpot's Content Assistant is ideal for businesses already using HubSpot for CRM and marketing who want integrated AI writing capabilities without managing separate tools.Pro tip:Use HubSpot Content Assistant with smart content features to create dynamic emails and landing pages that automatically personalize based on contact properties and behavior. Combine it with theHubSpot Breeze AI content writerto create SEO- and AEO-optimized content.Rating: 4.5/5Pricing:Several HubSpot subscriptions include HubSpot Content Assistant:Available with Marketing Hub Professional at $800/monthAvailable with Marketing Hub Enterprise at $3,600/monthAvailable with Content Hub Professional at $450/monthAvailable with Content Hub Enterprise at $1,500/monthChatGPTChatGPT is an AI-powered conversational assistant and content-writing tool that excels at generating, editing, and refining written content across multiple formats. It uses advanced language models (primarily GPT-4o, GPT-4o mini, and o1) to produce high-quality text for blog posts, marketing copy, social media content, emails, and scripts.Key FeaturesChatGPT understands context and maintains conversation history, allowing users to build on previous responses and refine content through natural dialogue.Supports multiple writing styles and tones from formal business communications to casual social media posts with the ability to switch styles mid-conversation.ChatGPT's memory feature (in Plus and Team plans) remembers user preferences, writing style requirements, and project details across sessions to generate more personalized content.What I LikeExceptionally user-friendly interface with a clean design and a minimal learning curve.Strong ability to maintain context and iterate on content without losing the thread.Versatile across content types from long-form articles to concise ad copy.In my opinion, ChatGPT is one of the best AI content writing tools because it provides versatile writing assistance, contextual understanding, and the ability to adapt tone and style to user requirements. I often use ChatGPT for its intuitive interface, quick content generation, and ability to iterate on drafts with follow-up instructions.When I entered my prompt, ChatGPT generated a well-structured draft with clear headings, natural flow, and appropriate tone. It also offered suggestions for improvement and allowed me to refine specific sections without rewriting the entire piece.I also downloaded the ChatGPT app on my iPhone 14 Pro. The app requires iOS 16.1 or later versions, and my phone supports it. It combines AI content generation with comprehensive SEO features, including keyword research, competitor analysis, NLP keyword suggestions, and real-time content scoring, all within the WordPress editor.Key FeaturesOne-click blog generation creates complete outlines based on keyword analysis and competitor research, streamlining content planning.Real-time content scoring evaluates SEO performance as users write content, with specific recommendations for improvement across 15+ ranking factors.NLP keyword analysis identifies semantically related terms and questions from platforms like Quora and Reddit to ensure comprehensive topic coverage.Competitor SERP analysis shows exactly what top-ranking content includes, revealing gaps and opportunities to create superior articles.37+ AI writing templates cover everything from blog intros and conclusions to product descriptions, ad copy, and social media posts using proven frameworks like AIDA and PAS.WooCommerce integration generates optimized product descriptions that highlight features and benefits while incorporating relevant keywords.What I LikeThe WordPress integration eliminates the need to switch between tools as everything happens in one place.Content scoring updates in real-time, showing exactly how changes affect SEO performance.Competitor analysis reveals specific content gaps that other tools miss.NLP keyword suggestions go beyond basic keyword research to include semantic variations and related questions.The free plan offers 2,500 words per month, making it easy to test before committing.In my experience, what sets GetGenie apart is the seamless WordPress integration. approvalsCMS integrationNot supportedDirect publishing and integrationsBest suited forExperimentation and flexibilityScalable content productionView all rowsView fewer rowsFrequently Asked Questions About AI Writing ToolsWhat is the best AI tool for authors?The best AI writing tool for authors depends heavily on individual goals and writing workflows. For marketers and teams already using HubSpot, Content Hub with Breeze by HubSpot may be a strong fit, as it combines drafting, editing, optimization, and publishing into a single workflow.It’s also a strong option for authors who need more structure, tone control, or brand consistency, particularly for professional publishing, team collaboration, and maintaining a consistent voice across large content projects.What is AI authoring?AI authoring is the use of artificial intelligence to assist in the writing process. It can help generate ideas, draft content, edit grammar, improve clarity, optimize SEO, and speed up repetitive writing tasks.Rather than replacing writers, AI authoring acts as a collaborative tool. Human authors still guide creativity, narrative direction, and critical judgment, while AI enhances efficiency, consistency, and scalability across different content formats.Is publishing AI-generated content legal?Yes, publishing AI-generated content is legal in most countries, including for blogs, marketing materials, and commercial use. However, legal frameworks governing copyright ownership vary by jurisdiction and the level of human involvement.Generally, meaningful human contribution, such as editing, structuring, and adding original insight, is required for copyright protection. Editorial oversight ensures originality, compliance, and ethical use, making AI a support tool rather than the sole author.Is ChatGPT the best AI writer?ChatGPT is one of the most versatile AI writing tools available, capable of handling a wide range of tasks, including drafting, brainstorming, summarizing, and conversational writing. Its flexibility makes it ideal for general use and experimentation.However, specialized AI writing tools often outperform ChatGPT in areas like SEO optimization, brand voice consistency, analytics, and team workflows. For professional publishing and marketing content, dedicated platforms like HubSpot may provide more targeted features.How do I choose the right AI writing tool?Choosing the right AI writing tool starts with understanding content needs. Consider whether the primary focus is writing blogs, academic content, marketing copy, or social media posts, as different tools excel in different areas.Other key factors include budget, SEO capabilities, collaboration features, integrations, and ease of use. Testing free trials and evaluating output quality can help teams find a tool that fits both the workflow and long-term goals.Do AI tools replace human writers?No, AI writing tools do not replace human writers. Instead, they enhance productivity by automating routine tasks like drafting, editing, and keyword optimization, allowing writers to focus on higher-level creative and strategic work.Human creativity, emotional intelligence, cultural understanding, and ethical judgment remain irreplaceable. The best content results from AI tools and human writers working together as complementary partners.Finding the Perfect AI Writing PartnerQuality content remains the cornerstone of an effective marketing strategy. While talented writers and marketers have long driven business success, AI authoring tools now offer powerful support to amplify their work.These AI content writing tools don’t replace human creativity and expertise, but can enhance it. By streamlining research, drafting, and editing workflows, AI-powered authoring tools free up creators to focus on strategy, originality, and authentic connection with audiences.Whether looking to scale content output, overcome writer's block, or simply work more efficiently, the AI authoring tools explored in this guide offer compelling capabilities worth testing.Try one that aligns with specific needs and see how it transforms the approach to content creation.Free AI Content WriterIdeate, create, and share content. AEO GUIDENavigate the AI revolution and with proven strategies to optimize your content for AI-powered discovery.Download for FreeUpdated:03/31/26When I first started working in content and weaving SEO into my strategy, I treated Page Authority like a report card: the higher the score, the better I was doing. It took a few humbling ranking losses to a competitor with a lower PA score to make me reconsider.Turns out, Page Authority is more of a compass than a finish line. In this post, I‘m breaking down what it actually is, how it’s calculated, what a good score looks like, and what you can do to improve it — so you can use it to guide your strategy, not just grade it.Table of ContentsWhat Is Page Authority?Is Page Authority a Google Ranking Factor?Page Authority vs Domain Authority vs PageRankWhat Is a Good Page Authority Score?How to Check Page AuthorityHow to Increase Page Authority Without Gaming the SystemFrequently Asked Questions About Page AuthorityFree AEO Guide: HubSpot's Guide to Answer Engine OptimizationNavigate the AI revolution with proven strategies to optimize your content for AI-powered discovery.How answer engines rank and choose contentPractical templates and checklistsAEO strategies that convert 27% of AI traffic to leadsReal examples from HubSpot's AEO implementationDownload for FreeLearn moreDownload for FreeDownload FreeAll fields are required.You're all set!Click this link to access this resource at any time.Download NowWhat Is Page Authority?Page Authority (PA) is a third-party metric created byMozthat estimates the relative ranking potential of a specific webpage on a 0-100 scale. A higher score suggests the page is more likely to rank competitively in search engine results pages (SERPs).SourcePA is scored on a 0-100 logarithmic scale, which means improvements become progressively harder to achieve the higher the score climbs. Moving a page from a PA of 20 to 30 takes far less effort than moving it from 70 to 80.Moz calculates Page Authority using a machine learning model trained on thousands of search result data points. The primary input is the quality and quantity of inbound links pointing to a given page. Other signals, including linking root domains and MozRank, also factor in.Pro Tip:Because PA is logarithmic, focus your energy on winning relevant backlinks rather than obsessing over moving the score a few points. The links drive the movement, not the other way around.One important clarification: PA measures a single page, not an entire website. If you want a domain-level signal, that is what Domain Authority (DA) is for. I'll cover the distinction in detail in the comparison section below.Is Page Authority a Google Ranking Factor?No. Page Authority is not a Google ranking factor. Google doesn‘t publish a Page Authority score, and it doesn’t use Moz's PA metric in its algorithm.Google has its own internalPageRank algorithm, which is historically associated with link analysis. PageRank is a different system entirely, and Google stopped publishing public PageRank scores in 2016. The name similarity between “PageRank” and “Page Authority” causes ongoing confusion in the industry, but they are unrelated.So why does Page Authority still matter? Because it correlates with rankings. Pages with strong PA scores tend to have strong backlink profiles — something Google does care about. PA is a proxy, not a cause. When a page has a high PA score, it typically signals that the page has earned credible links, which is a signal Google respects.What We Like:Using PA as a comparative benchmarking tool rather than an absolute target. If your competitor‘s ranking page has a PA of 55 and yours is 30, that gap tells you something actionable about your link-building opportunity. It’s the delta that matters, not the raw number.My experience has taught me that the most dangerous thing a team can do is set a PA target as a KPI. When PA becomes a goal rather than a diagnostic, teams start chasing score improvements through shortcuts, like acquiring low-quality links, that can harm long-term performance. Treat PA as context, not a scoreboard.Page Authority vs Domain Authority vs PageRankThese three terms are frequently conflated. Here is a clear breakdown of each, when to use them, and what distinguishes them.Page Authority (PA):Created by Moz. Estimates the ranking potential of a specific page. Scored 0-100. Updated regularly based on Moz's link index.Domain Authority (DA):Also created by Moz. Measures the relative strength of an entire domain rather than a single page. A site's DA score is influenced by the combined link equity across all of its pages.Learn more about Domain Authority here.PageRank:A Google algorithm historically associated with link analysis. Google uses PageRank internally, but it has not published public PageRank scores since 2016. PageRank is not a tool for practitioners to use; it is an internal Google signal.Here is a side-by-side comparison to make the distinctions clearer:Page Authority vs Domain Authority vs PageRankPage Authority (PA)Domain Authority (DA)PageRankCreated byMozMozGoogleWhat it measuresSingle page ranking potentialEntire domain strengthLink-based page importanceScale0-100 (logarithmic)0-100 (logarithmic)Not publicly disclosedIs it a Google ranking factor?NoNoHistorically yes (now internal)Best used forPage-level link comparisonDomain-level benchmarkingUnderstanding Google's link philosophyUpdatedRegularly via Moz's indexRegularly via Moz's indexInternal; not shared publiclyView all rowsView fewer rowsWhen to use page vs domain metrics:Use Page Authority when you are analyzing a specific URL, such as a blog post, landing page, or product page.Use Domain Authority when you are evaluating a site-level link-building strategy or comparing your overall domain strength against a competitor.Neither metric is superior; they answer different questions.Pro Tip:When evaluating link-building targets, check both PA and DA. A page with low PA on a high-DA domain is often a great acquisition target because the domain has strong equity but the specific page has room to grow.What Is a Good Page Authority Score?There is no universal answer, because Page Authority is a relative metric. A PA of 40 might be excellent for a niche B2B service page and completely insufficient for a highly competitive keyword in the finance or health space. Context determines what counts as a good score.That said, here is a general reference guide for interpreting PA scores:Page Authority vs Domain Authority vs PageRankPage Authority (PA)Domain Authority (DA)PageRankCreated byMozMozGoogleWhat it measuresSingle page ranking potentialEntire domain strengthLink-based page importanceScale0-100 (logarithmic)0-100 (logarithmic)Not publicly disclosedIs it a Google ranking factor?NoNoHistorically yes (now internal)Best used forPage-level link comparisonDomain-level benchmarkingUnderstanding Google's link philosophyUpdatedRegularly via Moz's indexRegularly via Moz's indexInternal; not shared publiclyView all rowsView fewer rowsI have seen pages with PA scores in the 20s outrank pages with PA scores in the 50s because the lower-authority page was a significantly better match for search intent. This is why PA is a signal, not a guarantee. A well-targeted, technically sound page with strong on-page optimization can punch above its PA weight.The most important benchmark is competitive, not absolute. Use tools like Moz's Link Explorer, Ahrefs, or Semrush to analyze the PA scores of pages currently ranking in the top three for your target keyword. That range becomes your practical target. You can also useHubSpot's Website AEO Graderto assess your overall site performance and surface gaps that could be affecting your pages' authority.Free AEO Guide: HubSpot's Guide to Answer Engine OptimizationNavigate the AI revolution with proven strategies to optimize your content for AI-powered discovery.How answer engines rank and choose contentPractical templates and checklistsAEO strategies that convert 27% of AI traffic to leadsReal examples from HubSpot's AEO implementationDownload for FreeLearn moreDownload for FreeDownload FreeAll fields are required.You're all set!Click this link to access this resource at any time.Download NowHow to Check Page AuthorityChecking Page Authority doesn't require any single tool. The goal is to assess ranking potential and link profile strength at the page level, and several platforms offer this data. Here is a tool-agnostic process:Identify the specific URL you want to evaluate.Copy the exact URL, not just the domain. PA is page-specific.Open a link analysis tool.Options include Moz Link Explorer, Ahrefs Site Explorer, Semrush Backlink Analytics, or Majestic. Most offer free limited queries or trial access.Enter the URL.Paste the full page URL into the tool's search bar and run the analysis.Record the PA score and note the number of linking root domains.The linking root domains count is often more useful than PA alone because it shows how diverse the link profile is.Repeat for competitor pages ranking for the same keyword.Run the same analysis on the top three results for your target keyword and note their PA and linking domain counts.Calculate the gap.If your page has a PA of 28 and the top-ranking pages average PA 48, you now have a directional link-building goal.Document and track over time.PA fluctuates as Moz refreshes its index. Track month-over-month rather than reacting to day-to-day changes.For ongoing SEO performance tracking,HubSpot's Marketing Hub SEO toolslet you monitor on-page optimization, keyword rankings, and content performance in one place, giving you the context to pair PA data with real ranking outcomes.Best For:Teams doing competitive content analysis should build a simple tracking spreadsheet that logs the PA, DA, and linking root domains of the top three ranking pages for every target keyword. Run this audit quarterly to identify which content gaps have grown and which pages are gaining ground.How to Increase Page Authority Without Gaming the SystemChasing shortcuts is the fastest way to undermine a page‘s long-term authority. Buying links, joining link schemes, or overloading pages with exact-match anchor text might bump your PA in the short term, but they’re also the kind of tactics that tend to attract algorithmic flags or manual penalties. Here is what actually works, based onSEO best practicesand durable signal improvement:1. Earnhigh-qualitybacklinks fromrelevantsources.PA is primarily a link-based metric. The single most effective way to increase it's to earn links from pages and domains that are themselves authoritative and topically relevant to your content.Tactics that work include original research (data studies, surveys, proprietary reports), creating genuinely useful reference content, building tools or calculators your industry will cite, and proactive outreach to sites that link to similar content from competitors.What We Like:Original data that makes your page a primary source. When a piece of content becomes the reference that others cite, the links compound over time without additional outreach effort.2. Strengthenyourinternallinkingstructure.Internal links distribute link equity across your site. A page buried without internal links receives none of the authority flowing through your higher-PA pages. Conduct areview of your ranking metricsalongside a crawl of your internal link structure to identify pages that deserve links but are not receiving them.The ideal internal linking strategy connects your highest-equity pages to the pages you most want to rank. If your homepage or cornerstone content has strong PA, make sure it links to your target pages with descriptive, keyword-relevant anchor text.Pro Tip:Run a site crawl using tools likeScreaming FrogorAhrefsand filter for pages with strong organic traffic but few internal links pointing to them. These are your quick wins for internal link equity transfer.3. Producecontentthatmatchessearchintent.A page that earns clicks, reads, and return visits signals quality to Google even if its PA is modest. Content that genuinely matches what searchers want tends to attract more organic links over time, which then drives PA upward.Before optimizing for authority, confirm the page is doing the basic job: satisfying the query. A page with excellent intent alignment and a PA of 35 will often outperform a page with mediocre relevance and a PA of 55.4. Keeptechnicalhealth inorder.Page Authority cannot do its job if the page has technical problems. Crawl errors, slow page speed, broken links, and poor mobile performance all suppress a page's ability to rank, regardless of its PA score. Audit pages for technical issues before investing in link building.Check for crawl errors and redirect chainsEnsure the page passes Core Web Vitals thresholdsConfirm the page is indexable (not accidentally blocked by robots.txt or noindex)Fix broken internal and external links on the page5. Update andimproveexistingpages.Refreshing content signals freshness and often earns new links as updated statistics or insights get cited. I've discovered that some of the fastest PA gains come not from new pages, but from significantly upgrading existing pages that already have some link equity. Adding new data, deeper analysis, or better multimedia can prompt existing linkers to update their references and new linkers to discover the page.6. Buildtopicalauthorityaround the page.Pages rarely rank in isolation. A single page on “email marketing” will rank more effectively if it's part of a cluster of related, well-interlinked content on email marketing broadly. Building a content cluster around a core topic distributes authority from supporting pages to the pillar page and signals to Google that your site has deep expertise in the area.Best For:Content Hub by HubSpot is built for this approach, enabling teams to create interconnected content clusters with clear pillar pages, topic coverage, and internal linking at scale.7. Prioritizelinkdiversity,notjustvolume.Ten links from ten different relevant domains are more valuable to PA than ten links from the same domain. Moz's model rewards linking root domain diversity. When building links, prioritize reaching new domains over accumulating additional links from sites that already link to you.Frequently Asked Questions About Page AuthorityHow often should you check page authority?Monthly tracking is a reasonable cadence for most teams. PA fluctuates as Moz refreshes its link index, which happens regularly. Checking daily or weekly creates noise and can lead to reactive decisions based on index crawl variations rather than actual link profile changes. For competitive tracking, a quarterly deep-dive audit is usually sufficient.Does internal linking increase page authority?Internal linking can help page authority, but not directly. When you link from one page to another, some link equity transfers to the linked page, which can raise its PA slightly. With that said, external backlinks are what actually drive PA in a meaningful way. Internal linking helps you get more out of the authority your site has already earned — it doesn't create new authority on its own.Can a page with low page authority still rank?Yes, frequently. PA is one signal among many. Pages with low PA regularly outrank higher-PA pages when they are a better match for search intent, have stronger on-page optimization, or face low competition. In niche topics with few authoritative pages, a PA of 15 or 20 can be more than sufficient to rank on page one.Should I compare page authority across industries?Comparing PA across industries is not particularly useful. A PA of 50 in the technology space may be competitive, while the same score would be below average in the news or finance sectors where major publishers dominate. Always benchmark PA within the actual SERP you are trying to compete in, not against some abstract industry average.Why is my new page's page authority so low?New pages start with a PA near 1 because they have not yet earned any backlinks, and PA is primarily a link-based metric. This is expected and normal. A new page won't improve its PA through on-page optimization alone. Earning the first few relevant backlinks, building internal links from established pages, and giving the page time to be indexed and crawled will all contribute to gradual PA growth over weeks and months.Ready to Build Pages That Rank?Knowing your Page Authority score is just the beginning — the real work is matching content to intent, building links with purpose, and keeping your technical foundation solid.
